ocr.text: The data on ownership types reveals that, under the header "Type of ownership," the national sample percentages and
corresponding number of health facilities to sample are as follows: for "Public facilities," the national sample is 62% with 121
health facilities; "Private not-for-profit" accounts for 18% with 35 health facilities; "Private for-profit" comprises 17% with 33
health facilities; and "HIV research clinics" represent 3% with 6 health facilities, resulting in a total of 100% and 195 health
facilities sampled.
Type of ownership  National sample (%)a  Number of health facilities to sample
Public facilities  62  121
Private not- for- profit  18  35
Private for-profit  17  33
HIV research clinics  3  6
Pattern  Students (n=60)  Villagers (n=60)
No.  %  No.  %
Stop drugs when symptoms disappear  17  28.3  24  40.0
Stop when tired of drug  8  13.3  11  18.3
Take some and leave the rest  10  16.7  8  13.3
Take all drugs even when symptoms disappear  25  41.7  10  16.7
Do not complete dose due to financial constraint  0  0.0  7  11.7
In a separate analysis of drug adherence patterns, the first table lists patterns with corresponding data for students (n=60) and
villagers (n=60): 17 students, or 28.3%, stop drugs when symptoms disappear, while 24 villagers, or 40.0%, do the same. Eight
students (13.3%) stop when tired of the drug, compared to 11 villagers (18.3%). Ten students (16.7%) take some and leave the
rest, while 8 villagers (13.3%) follow this pattern. Additionally, 25 students (41.7%) take all drugs even when symptoms
disappear, versus 10 villagers (16.7%). Lastly, no students (0.0%) do not complete the dose due to financial constraint, while 7
villagers (11.7%) report this issue.
